If only it wasn't so shiny... Overall this jumpsuit is comfortable, soft and well fitted. A little tighter on the top than on the bottom on my frame. It has a bit of stretch to it, but the material also has a sheen that gets extra shiny on any parts that fit a bit tighter. The unfortunate part is that it therefore shines & highlights areas that I'd prefer to detract from (such as the upper arm). The shiny quality of the fabric also makes it look cheap in my opinion. The fabric is ribbed and a bit heavy, so it has some warmth. Although the fabric could be better, the cut and design are great. The open square neckline is a little too va va voom for my office, but is perfect for a night out. The waist hits at the right spot and length is good for average height ladies. I'm only 5'1", but I can wear it with high wedges without having to have it hemmed. I'm usually a size 4-6 and the small fits well (although more tightly in the arms than I'd like). Although there's some room for improvement, it's a nice jumpsuit for the price.

Do NOT go by the size chart!! Overall this maternity dress is beautiful! I was going to follow the size chart by bust measurements and went with my gut instead I am originally a size 16 but sized up an 18 (size chart would’ve put me in a 22-24) to ensure my still growing belly/bust will have plenty room come March for baby shower and maternity pics. CAUTION the under slip is short for someone tall I’m 4’10” and it’s above my knees! The dress itself drags the floor but that’s what I wanted in this dress.

Nice silhouette, fits to size, bad zipper. Overall, a very nice dress. I liked the color, the shape, the thickness of the fabric (completely non-transparent despite the light color). It is a bit static, so you need to make sure you have an antistatic spray handy, especially in the dry weather. For reference, I am size 6-8, 27” waist, 34DD, 5’9”. Size M is a high-waisted long midi dress on me. The zipper is not made well, it seems to be a problem lately, all the size zippers are horrible. Fortunately, I can put it on without using it, but it makes the side look heavier, I might just take it out and sew in the gap. The fabric is comfortable and soft, but being fully synthetic I would not recommend wearing it for too long, bring something else, just in case. Still, it is very feminine and pretty comfortable recommend.

Beautiful, but neck was a little tight and racerback bra shows Overall, I did like this dress and it was very flattering. I am 5'3" and 130 pounds and bought a medium. I kind of feel like maybe a large would have been better. As you can see from the photos, my racerback bra is showing, and I did feel like I was being a little choked on the neck all night. I found myself pulling it away from my neck throughout the evening. Maybe a large would have given me more room. It did seems little tight. That being said, it was a lovely dress and again, looked flattering and was comfortable (except for the tight neck).

Runs small; see through Overall, I would say that this is an average dress at best, which is why I am giving it an average 3-star rating. The first thing I noticed was that the material is very thin, so it is see through. So you'll need to wear a slip or something underneath. Although the material is thin, it is decently comfortable to the touch. It's a smooth, stretchy material. And although the material is very stretchy, the sizing runs small. I typically wear a 2X or 3X in similar dresses like this from department stores, so I ordered the 24W. It fits more like a 1X. Because the material is stretchy it does fit, but it is clingy and I typically like to wear my clothes looser. So size up if you also prefer that. The bust area is quite large, but it does have a clasp sewn on to try to keep it shut. Finally, it is quite short on me, but I am also tall (5'11"). For these reasons, I am giving this dress an average 3-star rating.

For larger chests; thin material with no stretch Overall, I would say that this is an average dress, which is why I am giving it an average 3-star rating. The first thing I noticed was that the color is more of a green than an emerald green. I was hoping the color would be a bit darker for a fall/winter wedding. The second thing I noticed is that the material is thin, so you can definitely see through it in the light. As a result, you will likely need to wear a slip or something underneath this. Although the material is thin, it doesn't have any stretch to it. There is stretch to the elastic waist, but not to the material itself. So it's not super comfortable to wear for long periods of time. In terms of fit, I sized up (I typically wear a 3X and ordered the 4X). I'm glad I did that because of the non-stretch material, however the chest area is quite large. I'm going to have to stitch it so it does not hang loose (I'm a C cup). Finally, the style does pretty much look as pictured, however I would say the bottom has more of a high/low effect. The photos make it look like the slit goes quite high, but thankfully it is only right above the knee so it is still tasteful for a wedding guest dress. The length of the dress is a bit awkward, but I'm also tall (5'11"). It hits me right above the ankles, so it's not really full length or cocktail length. For all these reasons, I would say this is an average dress.

Darling dress, good fit, decent quality and a great price Overall, I’m very pleased. Fabric seems nice quality and is the accurate color and look of what I saw advertised. It fits very nicely (I ordered size small, I’m 5’6” and weigh about 115). Didn’t notice the zipper ‘till after I took the dress off, but the fabric stretches a bit, allowing me to pull it on and off. It looks nice when worn over the petticoat that I ordered (different seller). Ribbons are nice quality fabric. The waist ribbon looks a bit thin when tied in a bow. I ordered this dress as a gift for my daughter for her birthday, it was delivered in totally clear, see-through packaging…I have never before received an Amazon package in totally clear packaging. My daughter saw it clearly on the front porch, which was disappointing to me. I sure hope my daughter likes it! (If not, then I’ll wear it!). Definitely would buy again and recommend.

The perfect dress! Overall, the perfect dress, pleasantly surprised by the quality. Super lightweight. I got a size small, 5’4” 130lbs. The top was a teeny bit loose, so I bought double-sided tape and it stayed in place all night.

Sexy and dressy, very booby Overall, this a great dress if you want to show off the sisters. I’m 5’4” 150 and wear 8 or 10, in this case the 10 is a bit big (and too long, but every long dress is too long when you’re 5’4”). There’s some stretch so the 8 would work well. Looks as pictured, but it's pretty thin and very short Overall, this is a cute dress that looks as pictured. The material is a thin, almost chiffon-like material. The material itself doesn't have any stretch to it, but the entire back and waist is like ruched elastic material that does stretch. In terms of size, I would say it's pretty true to size or to size up one. I typically wear a 3X but ordered a 4X because I know online clothes tend to run smaller, and the 4X fits great. Only taking off one star for two reasons. One, it is VERY short. It says it is short, so I should have expected that though. I am also pretty tall (5'11"), so that could be why it looks so short on me. Two, the material is quite thin and see through. So you're going to have to wear a slip or something under it.

Pretty dress, a few caveats Overall, this is a very pretty dress, and would be nice to wear to a cocktail-dress event. I'm 5'9", about 170 pounds, and the Large fit me snugly. A few caveats though: The shape wear is nice a compressive without being overly so, but the dress itself is pretty thin. This is only an issue when it comes to the bust area, where I felt like it was pretty flimsy. The bust was also pretty large on me (I'm about a 36B), and gapped when I moved my shoulders. Also, neither the dress itself nor the pads provided any support - I would probably add a strapless bra underneath to help support and fill out. The slit at the side offers a ruched drawstring, but if you pull it up any amount, the compression shorts underneath show. You could just hike the one side up, but I'm not sure I'd want to deal with that all night long. Overall, a nice dress, but I'm not sure it's worth the full price of $119, maybe more like $75.

Tough fit if you’re a pear shaped person Packaged well and arrived timely. I ordered 3 colors and all were as pictured online. Beautiful dress, very light weight and well made considering the low cost. I’m 5’5”, 135lbs, an A-cup with most of my weight in my hips/booty/thighs. I typically wear a size small/4 on top and depending on brand a 4/6/medium on bottom. Got this in a medium/6 and it was a TIGHT squeeze to get it over my hips and it gave the impression of a significant tummy pooch even tho I don’t have much of one. The bodice was quite loose and gaped terribly in the chest/arm holes. It would have taken some tailoring to get it to fit up top, especially since I would have needed to move up another size to fit my hips. I will note the length was PERFECT for me in just 2-3 inch heels. I was not a fan of the material - found it itchy and the tiny sequins rubbed under my arms but I have super sensitive skin. Beautiful option for someone with narrower hips and more chest.

Stunning Color, Amazing Quality!! Packaging: Let's start with the packaging—it came in a vacuum-sealed bag, which was a pleasant surprise. It arrived in perfect condition, and the attention to detail in the packaging was a nice touch. Color: The color of this dress is stunning! It's exactly as pictured, and the rich hue complements various skin tones. I received several compliments on the color alone. Style: The dress exudes style with its V-neck, twist front, and ribbed knit design. It strikes a balance between elegance and casual, making it versatile for different occasions. Quality: The quality of the dress is top-notch. The ribbed knit is comfortable and feels substantial. The stitching is well done, and it held up perfectly after a few wears and washes. Overall Appeal: Overall, this dress is incredibly appealing. It's the kind of piece that makes you feel confident and put-together. It's suitable for both casual outings and more formal events. Sizing: The only drawback was the sizing. I'm 5'3" and 103 lbs, and the fit was not perfect for my body type. It was slightly loose in some areas, which affected the bodycon fit that I had hoped for. Also was a little longer than I hoped, but I can't fault the dress for this since sizing chart was accurate! In summary, this is a beautiful addition to any wardrobe. From packaging to color, style, and quality, it excels in many aspects. While it didn't fit me perfectly, I believe it's a great choice for those whose body types align more closely with the sizing.

Almost perfect ….. Pants fit well….I ordered them in 3 different colors. I’m 5’6” and weigh 114 lbs and I ordered a size small. This is true for the 3 colors I ordered (mocha, brown sugar and olive). I can’t speak to the other colors but I would assume it is the same.

Beautiful dress. Perfect dress for a holiday party. Very modest but still sexy. Great for curvy figures. Perfect dress for a maternity shoot!! The material is extremely stretchy and hugs you like a glove. It almost has the texture of fleece on the inside, so this is a thick material and is not see through. Also, it is surprisingly water resistant! (I stood in the lake for approx 10 minutes and my dress just floated on top of the water.) I am 5’6 and the hem rested just around my ankles. If I were in heels I would have been disappointed with the length, however, it didn’t bother me due to being in sandals. I ordered a medium. Pre pregnancy weight I am 135/140lbs , and now at 33 weeks pregnant I am 170. (Yikes. Still getting used to saying that!!) Another reviewer mentioned the cheap zipper, so I might as well do the same. My experience with the zipper being “cheap” has more to do with the stretching of the dress. Along the zipper, it seemed to bunch up in the back. I probably would have felt uncomfortable if I had my hair in an updo, or if I wore this to an event and not just for photos. But that’s just my opinion. Overall, for the price you can not go wrong!! I have received hundreds of compliments on this dress, and I don’t feel guilty for buying such a “glamorous” dress to only be worn once!
Buy it!!! Perfect dress for a wedding!! So comfy and stretchy. It is true to size- I got a medium for ref I am 5”2 135 lbs. try it.. you won’t regret it!

Perfect Fit!! And its beautiful!! Hot, loose, too long Pictures from my sisters wedding where I was maid of honor. This dress is actually really pretty. It’s a nice dress but I changed out of it as soon as I could. I had to make the top super tight or else it wouldn’t fit right but that caused issues because then my bra showed through. Also I’m average height for a woman but this dress was long on me. It stuck to my shoes and caused me to trip going down the aisle. My sister-in-law however is 5’2-3 this dress was super long on her and she even wore high heels making her nearly as tall as me. We wore these in early May so it wasn’t too hot, except the fabric it’s made out of makes you feel like you’re in a sauna. So I loved the dress, the way it looks at least but I definitely wouldn’t spend the money on this. If I could return it now I would.

I’m happy with my purchase! Pink in size S and Yellow in size M but I like the way the medium fits best. The dress will ride up, but it wasn’t as bad when I sized up. I’m 5’2 160. I like this brand and I’ll be purchasing again.

Beautiful and Elegant! Pleasantly surprised with this dress. After reading the reviews, I wasn't sure what to expect. This dress is amazingly comfortable, the fabric is thicker than expected, and very flattering. I'm 5'3" and weigh 115 lbs and it fits great (bust 34 D) . I ordered a size small. I bought it for a witch costume, but honestly I hope to wear it again at a more formal event. I love it and can't wait to wear it. The slip underneath does tend to rise and cause some wrinkling, but it's still stunning. I would suggest wearing shapewear underneath to smooth out areas. I don't have to alter it anywhere, not even the length. If you have a special event and want to stand out, this dress is an excellent choice. Really happy with it!

pretty and versatile dress Please don't mind the color in my pictures, my camera likes to brighten things and I didn't adjust before uploading, the dress color is true to the product pictures, burgundy. I ordered a small because I knew that being a bodycon it would stretch to fit my curves and thought that the waist might've been too big at medium (my measurements are 37/28/42 and I'm 5'3" tall) although I'm sure now that med would've been fine. The dress is made of a decently thick material that isn't sheer at all, but is still breathable and quite comfortable as well as forgiving. There is barely any ruching in this dress when it is fitted, but if it is a little loose, there's a little across the front right under the belly and top of thighs and also under the chest.

So gorgeous that people won't believe it's from Amazon, but with some flaws Please note that the color in my images is off due to lighting, the color of the gown is true to the listing. This dress is absolutely stunning in person. It's so, so flattering! The color is soft and elegant, and it's very comfortable on. The skirt is also very full. I tried to take pictures of it, but my arms simply aren't long enough to hold the elaganza. Now for the slight issues. It does have hard cups, so it won't suit people with smaller chests unless those are removed. My dress is a size 4 and I'm a 32DDD for reference. The cups also have a bit of a weird seam under them that I don't like the look of, and I think I'll attempt to remove them and smooth that seam. They're sewn in quite well, so I may have issues. Hahaha The lining isn't great, but it's still soft enough to not be scratchy. Overall, this dress is beautiful and well-made enough for me to be willing to alter it instead of returning it for the slight issues I had. It really is that pretty! It looks like it came from a bridal shop!

Beautiful dress, BUT...... PLEASE WASH BEFORE WEARING!! It's a beautiful dress but it bled on everything. I wore a white the faux fir jacket and it turned it pink! I had blonde braids and the tips turned red. I ironed it before I wore it and it stained my ironing board and iron. I wish I would have known that..I didn't read comments before ordering and I regret it. I DO NOT regret buying the dress I just wish I knew that. Also I am 206lbs and 5'6 I wish I would have ordered a xl opposed to a 2x. I will probably never wear this dress again because I had VSG surgery and am currently losing every every week Hope this helps someone

If I had a million dollars... POCKETS! Right there that makes this dress a win. But it wins for other reasons too. The surplice neckline makes this easy to get on and off. If you are nursing this would work for you. I have a long torso and this empire waist came a bit higher than some others so if I didn't actively adjust the dress by pulling it down, the smocked waist wanted to lay across the twins. That was mildly annoying but not a deal breaker because- POCKETS. And real pockets. Ones you can fit your whole hand into. I am 5'8 and this dress brushed the tops of my feet. The lace detailing is sheer but since it falls below the knee it is by no means immodest. Neither is the top of the dress even though it has a deep V in the front and back. I wore a balconette style bra and I did not adjust the back tie strap after pulling it from the bag. So, the front of the dress stays closed enough that you are not flashing anyone, and the back even though it comes a bit lower does not come so low that you could not wear a traditional style bra with it. The material is heavier than I expected. This gives it a nice flow and presence. You also do not have to be worried about this being see through. I am 5'8 and at the time of these photos I was 139 pounds. I ordered the large, and I am pleased with the fit and feel. I would happily suggest this dress to any pocket lover or just because.

Beatifully controls tummy! Popilush Shapewear Dress Sparkly Cocktail Dresses for Women Tummy Control: I've never found a dress that controls curves like this dress. I ordered the x-small and at first I didn't think I was going to slip it on. However, once I did, wow! It holds everything in! Love it. I'm 29"x26"x36", 5'2" and 108#. This dress fits better than a glove! Great Taylor Swift outfit! Wear for the Reputation Era. Great find!

beautiful dress, but very badly made!! positives: -this dress is made of thick material and doesn’t feel cheap. -it’s a nice length and fit snugly. (i got an XS, i’m 5’ 6” and 112 lbs) negatives: -it did not fit right! not because i got the wrong size, but because there was a measuring discrepancy in making this dress. the dress was noticeably lopsided, so one side had about an inch or so more fabric than the other. when i pulled down on the left side of the dress, the right side would tighten up. this made wearing it feel very awkward. didn’t feel right when i sat down or bent over. -there was a snag in the fabric, which is surprising since i only wore it for a couple hours and wasn't doing anything strenuous.

Nice dress Pretty and flattering, beautiful neckline without too much cleavage. Showed a little more arm than I had hoped, arms were a little weird honestly. Runs small and cheap fabric and cheap seams leaves a low quality look Product Color: Purple Item Size: Medium Fit: Runs Small My Height/Weight: 5’9” My typical Size: Med/Large, Dress size 10 Although I have gotten items from the PRETTYGARDEN brand before, this seems below their typical quality. Cute sweater with bulky shoulders and extra long sleeves Pros: - The fit on the torso/bust and waist of this transitional sweater is flattering, causing the eye to see a waistline curve - Nice range of color choices. The white is bright and timeless. - The neckline is elongating and versatile because of the curved ”V” shape that the semicircular front pieces form - Fabric is soft and stretchy - Sweater front is banded, so it has a smooth/flat/unlined finish and will retain its’ shape well over time Cons: - Fit of the drop sleeve heads is overly generous, resulting in linebacker shoulders - The sleeves are exaggeratedly long (about 4” too long) for me - The ribs on the fabric are chunky, and this isn’t as flattering as a finer knit fabric Tips: - The brand and product care tags distort the shape of the left side seam. Kudos to the brand for having these sewn ONTO the seam instead of into the seam because they can be removed without having to repair the garment. After tag removal the seam will remain distorted, but can be easily re-shaped by spraying or sprinkling enough water on it to saturate the distorted section, pat it straight/smooth and let it dry flat. - Because it can’t be ironed, the same saturate and dry flat treatment will remove any residual wrinkles from shipping the product. - The primary fiber in this sweater is viscose (aka rayon) so the sweater will be prone to shrinkage. For best long term care, after washing in gentle cycle dry flat. Rating: For me this item is a 3* because of fit issues. I am 5’2”, 135# with a 42” bustline. 34” waist and wear a 34 G bra. The size chart would have put me in a size XL. I ordered the size L and could have gone down to the size M.

Just Kind Of Okay Pros: I like the color of the red - not too fire engine and not burgandy. Comfortable when it's on. Fabric has some weight to it - think 70's double-knit polyester.Cons: The pieces that form the belt hang from the front of the dress - like an elephant's trunk. I assume they are supposed to cross each other and tie in the back - they were too short to bring around to the front to tie. But when you cross them and pull to the back, it creates some awkward bunching in the front just under your chest. I tried to photograph from different angles to show this.The length might be a pro or a con depending on what you are looking for. I ordered a L. I am 5'6" tall, and this dress is just above my ankles, so if you are short, it might be a long dress on you. I imagine I will wear it, but I don't think it will be a dress I grab every time I look in the closet.

Beautiful & comfortable dress for short & plus size women. Pros: Beautiful dress! Glitters so pretty in the light. The sequined hemline is extra gorgeous! No snags or loose sequins. Fits very comfortable, the under skirt is a stretchy material. Great dress for a plus size girl and under 5'5". No alterations required. I wore this to our company Christmas party and received compliments all night! I was very comfortable and able to move and dance without stepping on the hem. The material is cool and wears comfortable, it wasn't heavy and I didn't feel hot or overheated wearing it. So glad I discovered this on Amazon. Wonderful dress except neckline Pros: Gorgeous dress that I really wanted to keep and wear. Very flattering and elegant, gorgeous peacock color (dark blue) true to the web picture. I am 5'8", 35-28-40 143lbs and ordered a small. It is the perfect size for me. The dress moves beautiful as you walk. The cons: If only the neckline was tighter! It was gapping in a weird way and I do not feel my neck is scrawny. The buttons are impossible to close due to the elastic that is way too short and tight. The weight of the buttons makes the neckline dip a but in the back: needs way smaller buttons. The slits on the sides are the right height, but seams were uneven which creates wrinkles all along the 4 sides. For just under $40 it is still a gorgeous dress but it needs alterations and I do not have the time for it. So sad!

OK LADIES here’s what you needa know PROS: got so many compliments on this dress! For reference, I am 5ft4in 140lbs and cup size B. It’s very silky and I like how it doesn’t wrinkle up. Not see-through. And looks like a unique quality dress. It IS a Very comfortable dress, but it does hug your curves. From the front, it looks very flattering but if I turn sideways, It emphasizes my mommy tummy. For that reason I did wear spandex underneath just suck in my rolls a bit. CONS: I did have to wear it with heels because I’m petite, Here in the picture I’m barefoot so you can see the length is too long. When you bend down too much cowl neck does droop down Exposing your bra a tad , So you either Have to be aware of it and hold it with your hand or wear a bandeau type thing to avoid flashing anyone .

Could use a few improvements, but.. PROS: Honestly, it’s cute and flattering for the price, and a night lightweight velvet material. Color is great in person, dress feels good. No issues with seams or quality. CONS: Soo not sure if it it’s mentioned anywhere, but this dress is literally put on like a robe. Took me 3 tries to figure out how to properly tie it cuz I’m a dummy. But… I’m gonna be wearing spandex underneath, because I got this for a wedding and I don’t wanna give anyone a show while I’m dancing! ☺️ Along with that, I’m going to need a cami up top, as others have mentioned it doesn’t cover well (I’m a 36C cup). Other Notes: I’m 5’3” & 150 lbs and am usually a L in most American made dresses. I got a M here and have more than enough room, maybe could have even gotten a S for shorter length. The dress is warm! Best for winter months, will look great for holiday parties this year 🥳

It’s fine Pros: It fit perfectly, although it was suggested I order a medium I read another review of a lady with a similar build and so I ordered a small. It fits exactly the way I’d like. Reference: (I’m 5’10, 145pounds, a/b cup). I will where this most likely with a crop sweater and be content. Cons- My dress has a awful smell and I fear washing it because the material well is cheap. It already is fraying from the seem at the top where the strap connects to the dress which again indicating how cheap this is actually made with cheap materials. Honestly not worth the price paid. I am going to just keep it because it will work for what I want but if I had bought it in person I would not have paid more then $10 for it. The front part with the drapery over the chest is also difficult to get to sit right and stay. Again I will where a sweater over the top not a big deal but for others it might be a deal breaker. 4/10 stars.

Awesome Dress, Great Value! PROS: This is a great dress! I wore it to my bridal shower and l will probably wear it as a reception dress for my wedding. I ordered a white XS and it fit perfectly (i'm generally between a size 0 and 2, but I would check the chart they provide either way). I love the neckline (you do need a bra that works with this; I had a bra that readjusted to a halter, but I still needed to hook the front straps together--it wasn't too big of a deal, though), the asymmetrical hemline drapes well (I'm only 4'9, but with 3 inch heels, the back of the dress just grazed the floor), the lace is beautiful, the material soft, and the zipper zips without struggle. I got so many compliments--people couldn't believe I bought it online for such a great price! CONS: Nothing...if I have to say something, there was this weird stain in the inside of the dress when I first got it. But it was so tiny and unimportant, it didn't matter. Thought I would just mention for completeness' sake. I wish I had more excuses to wear this dress tbh....buy it!

Simply lovely! Purchased this dress for our engagement photos. I was little nervous because I’m 5’ 2” and wasn’t sure if it would be too long for me. But looking at the reviews and seeing it on women my height made me feel a bit more confident. And because “Operation: Trim The Fluff” is still ongoing, I’m between sizes. I went with Medium because I didn’t want anything to cling unflatteringly. Being short, I was also worried that I would look like a wine-colored blob, but that wasn’t the case! The wrap bodice is really flattering (especially for someone like me who’s a member of the IBTC). With heels, the length was perfect, IMO. It’s also a great dress for twirling. It moves really well. If you’re getting photos in this, definitely make it move by twirling, flicking the dress, etc. I got soooo many compliments on our engagement photo previews about how it “moved”. I WILL say, the fabric is kind of thin (not see-thru thin, especially since the color is dark), but it won’t keep you warm. It also wrinkles pretty easily. When it arrived, I did a round of steaming to get the folding creases out of it and then steamed it again before the shoot. I’m looking forward to wearing it out on a date night when I want to “look like a girl”.

Cute But Runs Small Really cute dress with a nice drapes to it that gives the dress an elegant look. The fabric is of a nice quality for the price and has a good weight to it. I did find this dress to run a bit small. I ordered the XS and the measurements for the bust should be 33 inches. I only measure 31 inches on a good day, with me dropping to 30.5 most of the time and I am an A cup. This dress was so tight in the bust. I can honestly say I don't think that has ever been a problem for me. The rest of the measurements of this dress are off too. It was definitely too small overall. I ordered the XS and I am 5'10, 118 lbs, 31A-25-34 for reference. With a tulip hem, the fit in the waist and hips HAS TO be pretty much spot on because of the way it is cut and drapes. If you feel the split is way too high and the other side doesn't cover as it should that means it is too small. And when you couple that with a dress that was cut just a bit too small on the under flap, you will end up showing all your goodies. So if you want to take a chance on this dress, size up.

Great quality, reasonable price Really great quality for a reasonable price! For reference, I’m 5’10”, 160lbs, 32C, and got a medium. I was nervous about the top being too big, cause I typically like a secure fit and get a small in things like sports bras. But it actually fits quite well! The top is a just smidge loose for my liking, but it’s not actually falling down, I’ll just have to readjust every now and then. There’s also a strip of rubber at the top to prevent it from going anywhere. The rest of it fits really nicely - not tight at all, and the quality is thicker, silkier, and sturdier than I expected, while still being lightweight and not too warm. It’s a beautiful color but a bit see-through around the lighter parts, however it’s nothing that some nude underwear can’t solve. I did expect the length to be a bit shorter than on the model - it hits a few inches above my ankle since I’m on the taller side. No weird smells, adequately packaged, and it shipped so quickly (bought on a Friday, arrived Monday)! If you’re on the fence, it’s totally worth buying this - it’s really cute, very comfy, and a reasonable price!

Amazon for the win!!! Recently, I was invited to a black-tie event last minute. While I did end up running around to a couple stores and found another option, I thought I’d order this dress as a back-up to see what I thought because the reviews looked so good. Boy - am I glad I did! I lost 40 pounds in the past year and the other option I had found made me feel like an old lady - super covered up. This dress was the perfect elegant balance. The cleavage is covered and it is kind to a bit of a belly and hips. The fabric is forgiving and not clingy nor easily wrinkled. I also appreciated the length. I’m 5’6” and was able to wear 1.5-2” heels and slippers that were given out to walk around in without tripping on the hem of the dress or having it look too short. And can we talk about the price?!! The value for this dress is ridiculous!!! I wish I had occasions to buy it in other colors, let alone wear it more! Overall, I think it’s a very flattering dress that made me feel comfortable and confident for my first time in a dress like this in years!

Simply stunning is how I feel wearing this beautiful dress! It’s been a long time since I’ve encountered a dress that flatters my fuller figure to this degree, and I love its elegant style. I’m 5’7” and normally wear a size 16/XL, but decided on the XXL for this dress as the numbers in the size chart matched mine best. Definitely consult the provided measurements when deciding what suits you because they are accurate in my estimation; there’s no way I would have fit into the 16/XL size.There isn’t much stretch to the lace top as it’s obviously formfitting. A long, hidden zipper down the length of the back helps you get into the dress, but you’ll probably need assistance getting it zipped. The lace sleeves are fairly snug but not constrictive, and the satin ribbon at the waist is sewn into the garment. I like the ruffle of the overlayed skirt, and its asymmetrical effect is pretty, too. Overall, there’s not a single thing I don’t like about this dress—it’s even machine wash (with the use of a laundry back to protect the lacy material) and line dry.

True to size, maybe even too big Size down or stay true to your size, even if you’re between sizes, but DO NOT size up. I had to be sewn in and still had to have the corset back tightened as far as it could go because it was so large on me. Keep in mind, at the time I was 225 lbs. The top was also so large that we had to fold it and pin it in place, even so that the sleeves still slipped through out the night. I’d say this dress is very much well suited for bigger chested women (which I am not). Be ready to have to make major adjustments if you size up. Also if you’re ordering for a big event like I did, don’t wait to order. This took almost 3 weeks to get to me and I was terrified I wasn’t going to get it in time for my event. Over all if I bought again, I would buy my actual size or even size down. 10/10 everyone loved it other wise and I felt like a princess.

Recommendations: I recommend wearing a push-up bra with a little space or a gap in the chest area because the overlapping in the top chest area will show the bra. Never loved a maxi dress so much in my life!! So I am not a big dress girly. I got this maxi dress because I was planning on making my baby bump a disco ball for Halloween (I ended up wearing the dress and putting some stick-on gems on my face). However I really love this dress, when I took it out of the bag I was surprised that it had some weight to it but it wasn't too heavy. It's a great length, I got a small and I'm 127 pounds and 5'3". I'm also 33 weeks pregnant so my belly definitely stretched the dress and made the grey a little see through. Not enough to where I couldn't go to my SIL's Halloween party, but I could see my tattoos through the dress with my bathroom light on while I was getting ready. Definitely need seamless nude underwear and preferably a nude bra at least with the grey dress since I can't speak for the other colors. I haven't washed it a bunch yet so I don't know how sturdy the dress is, but I know it's definitely versatile for lounging at home or to dress up. I'm looking forward to seeing how it fits and if it's less transparent once I'm no longer pregnant, but I do plan on wearing this dress or getting another color/style of this same dress for my baby shower in a week!

So i am only 5ft 0in. So i was nervous about this but it fit perfect! It’s just the right length so i can wear flats or cowboy boots! I got a size large and it fit perfect in my chest area. Sheer and a bit wrinkled So I got this top because it appeared to be pretty solid and not sheer; however that’s not the case it is quite sheer and will need a camisole under it to avoid too much exposure. The blouse is very well made and I love the sleeves and how light weight it is, it will be a nice summer time shirt, given that it is so sheer and light. The only disappointment I have is the care instructions it can be machine washed but it needs to be hung to dry. Unfortunately when I did this some of the laundry detergent remained on the shirt and it came out pretty wrinkled. I can use a wet cloth to remove the soap residue but I’m concerned with using an iron because the material is so delicate. I may try a quick tumble in the dryer on low to see if that works. I should also mention that I’m 5’5” and 150lbs and my usual size is a medium, I ordered a medium in this shirt and it fit quite well, so it’s pretty true to size. For the most part with a little TLC I think this blouse will be nice to wear and look good overall.

true Fit with no issues So i had a wedding to go to and i had order a dress from a specialty store for plus size women I am an average 22/24 and im 5'8 I got it 2 days before the wedding and when i tired it on it did not fit even though i had ordered from them before. Now i was in panic mode and needed a new dress ASAP so my sister sent me a link for this dress and saw the reviews and order it next day all along hoping for the best. WOW turns out this dress was a true fit for me. I did have to pin the front of dress because it pop open but that was an easy fix. I did get a lot of compliments, the best ones where from my hubby, when i was asked i told everyone that it was my fancy bag dress and no one believed me that it was from amazon.

Decent quality, but not for me. So I love this style of dress. The quality of this is good but it shows every lump. I’m fairly slender, but we all have “bra fat” no matter what, it seems. Anyway, I choose not to highlight my bra fat and this dress certainly does that. It’s flattering enough aside from that but I’d have to wear a shrug or jacket every single time. For reference I’m a size 5 jeans, 5’7”, 135lbs and this is a medium in the pics. I suppose shape wear would help but I don’t want to feel so restricted so it’s going back.

Perfection! So I normally wear 2X and I am 6 foot 3, but I bought the 3X and I’m glad I did! It fit perfectly! The color was exactly as pictured. The fabric is very thick. The only thing this dress is missing is pockets! It does have a zipper on the side. The corset strings on the back are a great design so you can cinch your waist a bit. I’m going to buy this again in different designs for date nights!

SOFT and flattering So I ordered two sizes...small and medium. The small in green, and the medium in burgundy/wine. I am 150 lbs, 5'7", 34" chest (AA/A cup) 28" waist and 41" hip, to give you an idea of fit. The burgundy was OK but I didn't feel like it was terribly flattering. Looked a little baggy even though it technically flowed well. The small (green) worked very well for me and eliminated the vaguely baggy look I'd seen in the wine. Fabric is soft and I don't feel any scratching (a common problem for me when there's ruching/twists like the ones at the wrist and ribcage). Fabric is heavy and a nice weight as well as being very stretchy. It flows well. Like some commenters mentioned, the pockets do create a little extra width in the hip area, but I noticed that effect significantly more in the M than the S. Fabric is fairly warm, though I can't speak to breathability (bought this specifically for a fall event). Definitely recommend!

Unfortunately not flattering So I typically wear a size 12 these days (I had a baby this year, so I’ve been trying to add pieces to fit my new postpartum shape). I’m around 5’6”. This dress just wasn’t flattering when I put it on. I am sure I could have fit in a large, but I got an XL because I prefer my clothes not to be as tight since I had a baby. Anyway, I found the dress to just highlight everything I didn’t want it to highlight. I figured with the ruching on the sides, it would be flattering at the stomach, but it wasn’t. I even put on shapewear and heels and it somehow fit worse? I felt it really highlighted my stomach (which to be honest, I’m not that sensitive about and don’t feel looks horrible despite having had a baby this year). Also it weirdly made my breasts look small, which is bizarre, because they are not. The color also was a touch duller than I thought it would be. Not as deep an emerald color. I was excited when I saw it though, as it looked nice and the fabric wasn’t too heavy, nor thin and cheap feeling. Unfortunately, it is not to be and I’m going to return it.

As described, apart from the tiny armhole issue So I'm 6'1 and 175lbs at the moment, 36DD/ 34F, and measurements are 39-30-39. I ordered a size up for me, a 12 in mint, fit everywhere but the armpit/ shoulders. I am broader up top than most and athletic so that may be part of it, but I have never had a dress ride so high in the chest and arms before, and honestly don't have too much girth there so not sure what's up. I have ordered the same size in other styles from this brand without issue, so I think is the style of the top, probably best for shorter torsos/ smaller chests and slim shouldered ladies. Otherwise it's flattering and flowy so I gave 3 stars. Just might be to include an arm measurement in their chart for this one. I did just order another style that's like this but chiffon up top, hopefully one of those fits better, but based on other reviews the 14 in this particular style will have a similar issue, plus be to big for my waist. Had to return and not reorder unfortunately.

Great long flows dress So so comfy! It is quite long, I can’t wear flats (I’m 5’2) but great for almost any occasion, dress it up or down. Ties at the should so you can easily adjust as needed. Wore this to a tennis match in Paris and received many compliments.

My kid absolutely loves this dress So the dress is great quality & beautiful, unfortunately we followed the recommendation from reviews to size up & its to big. And even worse we can’t send it back in time. We already spent an extra $30 to get it here in time. But my kid loves the dress so much she has to make a decision to either wear it as is (we can get it to fit by lacing the corset as tight as possible, but you loose that pretty corset look & there’s still a gap); or try and send it back for the correct size & wear a different dress to prom but have the dress in the right size for future wear. So unless the dress sizing is inconsistent in different sizes, I’d rec getting your normal size. My kid is normally somewhere between an XS & small & usually goes with size small. She’s petite (5.2 & 1/2, 102lbs) but has a larger bust for her size (30D). That’s why we often go a bit larger to make sure the bust isn’t to tight. I really don’t think that would be an issue with this dress bc of the adjustable corset. So consider that when it comes to sizing, you have a lot of wiggle room bc the corset goes below the hips. So quality: im impressed. Usually when faced with an item from multiple sellers it’s kind of a c.rap shoot on whether the quality will be good. Sometimes like with this dress you get impressive quality, other times not. Normally I wouldn’t risk it for an important event, but this dress was the only one that fit my very picky kid’s needs. All the American dresses with quicker shipping time (which would have given us time yo return the dress if it didn’t fit the way we wanted), were either to s.exy, to long, to short, and most were sleeveless or had to deep a v neck. Also most didn’t fit her aesthetic, being to sophisticated or to frou frou. My kid likes clothes that have a bit of a cottagecore vibe. The dresses from China were the only ones that fit what she wanted. This dress was the only one that came in a color she wanted, had the style she wanted, wasn’t super short or super long (if you’re shorter this isn’t a mid length dress, it’s ankle length, which was fine, she just worried about tripping over the dress). So when the dress got here and was so well made & the fabric so nice, we were very relieved. There’s only so much you can trust Amazon reviews. So in conclusion we bought a size 6 but should have gotten a size 4. Maybe even a size 2, but I think a 4 would work. I would def go with the size that normally works best for you, & if possible order well ahead of time so if the fit is wrong you can try and return it for the correct size. But the quality is excellent. $65 + shipping is a great price for this dress. (We paid closer to $100 bc of expedited shipping, but I think it’s worth that much if this is the style of dress you want.) Edit: my kid is so in love with the dress it’s more important to have it in the correct size and it’ll be her new go to fancy dress. It really is lovely and if you don’t need it fast, the price is incredible for the quality and look. So we’re going to return it for a smaller size. Fingers crossed we get the right size. My kid is petite so I’m worried a size 4 will still be a bit big but I also don’t want to buy a to small size. So here’s hoping we get the correct size this time.

She’s petite (5.2 & 1/2, 102lbs) but has a larger bust for her size (30D). That’s why we often go a bit larger to make sure the bust isn’t to tight. I really don’t think that would be an issue with this dress bc of the adjustable corset. So consider that when it comes to sizing, you have a lot of wiggle room bc the corset goes below the hips. So quality: im impressed. Usually when faced with an item from multiple sellers it’s kind of a c.rap shoot on whether the quality will be good. Sometimes like with this dress you get impressive quality, other times not. Normally I wouldn’t risk it for an important event, but this dress was the only one that fit my very picky kid’s needs. All the American dresses with quicker shipping time (which would have given us time yo return the dress if it didn’t fit the way we wanted), were either to s.exy, to long, to short, and most were sleeveless or had to deep a v neck. Also most didn’t fit her aesthetic, being to sophisticated or to frou frou. My kid likes clothes that have a bit of a cottagecore vibe. The dresses from China were the only ones that fit what she wanted. This dress was the only one that came in a color she wanted, had the style she wanted, wasn’t super short or super long (if you’re shorter this isn’t a mid length dress, it’s ankle length, which was fine, she just worried about tripping over the dress). So when the dress got here and was so well made & the fabric so nice, we were very relieved. There’s only so much you can trust Amazon reviews. So in conclusion we bought a size 6 but should have gotten a size 4. Maybe even a size 2, but I think a 4 would work. I would def go with the size that normally works best for you, & if possible order well ahead of time so if the fit is wrong you can try and return it for the correct size. But the quality is excellent. $65 + shipping is a great price for this dress. (We paid closer to $100 bc of expedited shipping, but I think it’s worth that much if this is the style of dress you want.) Edit: my kid is so in love with the dress it’s more important to have it in the correct size and it’ll be her new go to fancy dress. It really is lovely and if you don’t need it fast, the price is incredible for the quality and look. So we’re going to return it for a smaller size. Fingers crossed we get the right size. My kid is petite so I’m worried a size 4 will still be a bit big but I also don’t want to buy a to small size. So here’s hoping we get the correct size this time.

Very impressed, LOVE it!!! So we’re actually having a small ceremonial reception so I thought I’d go for something shorter and more low key. I thought I’d just try this one on for fun. I honestly got it with the expectation of returning it, but when I put it on I didn’t want to take it off. I may have had it on for about an hour lol. I was also looking for something to hide my mom tummy and almost didn’t think it was possible. The corset really works wonders. You will only need someone to help with lacing it for you. Oh and for me the bonus is that I won’t even need to do a wardrobe change for dancing because the skirt is just hitting the floor. With heels, the length is perfect. For reference, I am 5’7 and 158lbs. I ordered the size 8. No alterations are needed and no bra or shape wear. The padding is very firm. You could add a hoop skirt if you’d like. Needless to say, this dress has really surpassed my expectations. I want to say more but overall, I am very excited for my husband to be to see me in this dress. We just may need to call this a wedding after all!

My second of these dresses-great for night out/ traveling So, I bought the olive in small and it fits but is a “tighter” or more fitted version of this dress. I'm pretty happy with it. So, I decided to be rather picky and take forever choosing my dress for my winter dance. A lot of the dresses i wanted wouldn't arrive on time which is honestly disappointing. I didn't think I could pull off a tighter dress, but God darn! I look so good!!! The back isn't as amazing as the front, but that might just be the shape of my back and stuff. The zipper works good, but it does kinda bunch up a little bit while wearing the dress. It doesn't ruin the look or anything, just kinda made me self conscious while trying it on, but oh well, I can't see it while I'm dancing doesn't matter. 1 thing that did concern me was the way it came. It came pretty fast, like 3 days or something so YAY! but It literally came in like a zip lock bag type thing with a package sticker on it. So it didn't come super packaged which is a little concerning, but it's all good. The sewing by the zipper looks pretty okay, not bad at all, just not the nicest prettiest job. The sleeves are nice, they are long enough and not to tight or loose on the cuff, but definitely slide up when i put my arms up. The dress itself doesnt ride up much unless im like going up stairs. Also, for reference, im around like 5' 3" 5' 5"ish, and around 135 lbs. I got a small, its not to tight, its rather comfy but the sleeves do ride up when my arms go up. I hope my date likes the dress, I do, I recommend it. Also I hope you like my fuzzy nerd socks, it's a little bonus in my review🙃 I love those socks so much!

Don't wash, it bleeds! So, I wore this for my bachelorette party and got raving reviews. Fits well and appears well made So, I’ve been wearing the same velvet jumpsuit to my husband’s annual company Christmas party for about three years now. I needed a change! It feels comfortable, not scratchy and even though it says “mini” it’s really not that short (on me anyway - I’m 5’4”). The one sleeve design adds some interest. The fit is a bit generous. I usually size up to M because of how small clothes on Amazon are, but this is a bit loose in the waist. But I figure it’ll be good and not too restrictive. If you want a skin tight fit, size down.

Last minute rescue dress! So, knowing it would only cause stress, I attempted to make my own wedding dress. And I waited until the last minute. When I realized on Sunday that my dress would not make my happy on Friday's wedding, I ordered this and a few other options. I paid for the expedited shipping, but I still didn't have the guarantee that it would make it in time. Not only did it arrive two days early, the fit was perfect, and I just loved it. I have received so many compliments on this dress. Sizing--I'm 5'7", 275 lbs, and I ordered the 26 based on measurements (I normally wear a 22). Do order by measurements, not size. It hit the ground on me, so it might need a hem for someone shorter. It would be an easy hem, though. No advanced sewing skills needed.

Showstopper Sophisticated and tasteful, and very attractive! This dress checks all the boxes. I followed the size chart and it fit perfectly. The only minor complaint I have is that I wish the material was just a tad thicker. I wore shape wear underneath the dress, and you can see the lines of the shape wear through the material. You can also see the lines of my bra. It wasn’t a huge deal for me, but I wish those lines were a little more hidden. Other than that, I really like everything about it. It hugs my body without being too restrictive. The material is soft and pretty lightweight… it was a little wrinkly straight out of the package, but those wrinkles smoothed out when I put it on. The ruching is so pretty! It’s at the right shoulder and the left side of the body, which makes the overall look even more regal. The dress fell a couple inches above my ankles (I’m 5’6”) and it moved very well with me throughout the day when I wore it to work. I absolutely adore the simple design of this dress and highly recommend it. You’ll get a lot of compliments!

Sorry about the white marks, i'm not tryna spread pictures of my people over the internet. This is a great dress, all six bridesmaids used it in my sister's wedding. The material is great, it is a little stretchy and bouncy, but has a good solid feel to it. The weight of the dress is great, it hangs really well. With a pretty good breeze, the dress doesn't billow, only the end of the skirt flows in the wind, so it looks great. You need to wear seamless underwear with it because you can definitely see underwear lines through the material, though it is not super thin material, it is just very form-fitting. Because of the slippery material, after wearing for a while, all of our dresses slipped a little where we tied them, but it was easily fixed and didn't change how i liked the dress. The skirt is very long. I'm 5'8" and bought a medium, i tied the waist just below my breasts and the skirt hung all the way to the floor, almost to the point of dragging. The straps for tying are very long, almost two times longer than the skirt itself, which leaves a lot of room to make sure you can tie it however you want then wrap the straps around yourself or leave them hanging. The bandeau that was sent with the dress is fine. One of the women had to buy another bandeau because the one that was sent with her dress was sewn lopsided and crooked and could not be used. For the rest of us it was just fine. The dress worked for the pregnant woman in our group, because the skirt is long it went over her stomach without affecting the length very much (she was close to her due date, but also she is very slim). The dress worked for the nursing mother in our group, though it would depend how you tie it how much of a hassle it will be. I like the dress a lot, I haven't worn it since the wedding, but I'm sure I could wear it anywhere because it is simple and pretty elegant.

Photographs beautifully. Specifically purchased this for my maternity shoot and it was perfect. The photographer fell in love with it and I got so many compliments at the beach and when I posted them. The day of my photoshoot was super windy but even though it bubbled up I didn’t mind because it showed the design as it blew in the air. It has such beautiful details! I ordered a size small and it fit my 8month pregnant belly just fine. I do think it might be a little wide for someone who isn’t pregnant. I plan to make mommy and me pictures once I have my daughter and hope it will fit just as nice in my postpartum body. I’m 5’1 and it was just a little bit long but not a big deal. I held it up to walk and for the pictures it wasn’t an issue. It wasn’t see through but regardless I wore nude undergarments to be safe. I have also already washed it since the bottom was full of sand and darkened. I washed it in delicate and hung dry and it looks good as new no issues. It’s a great dress, if you’re considering it just buy it! I loved it.

This is the Second “72 Styles Dress” I Have Purchased in a Month! The “72 Styles Infinity Dress” in a beautiful Teal (greenish blue) color arrived promptly and was better than described online. I chose this knee length dress to wear to an upcoming party. It would make a beautiful bridesmaid’s dress as well. I knew from the start that this dress would be a winner. Just three weeks earlier I had chosen a full length “72 Styles Infinity Dress” in Blush Pink color to wear as a bridesmaid in a wedding. Four other bridesmaids wore the identical dress in the same color. Yet everyone looked beautiful and unique as they created their own personal style of bodice type with an infinity of different styles to choose from (please see photos.) I have designed and sewn custom Bridal Gowns and formal dresses for many years. The truth today is that quality fabric by the yard is very expensive. If on a budget, then this high quality "72 Styles Infinity Dress" is a 5*+ winner. It has many exceptional features at a very affordable price. PROS: * High Quality Materials and Construction - The exquisite soft hand and hefty richness of the "Teal” fabric was immediately impressive. I also noticed that the dress was amazingly wrinkle free right out of the bag and box. Within 24 hours of hanging in a humid bathroom area, it was entirely wrinkle free. * Unbelievably comfortable to wear in any style that you choose! * Excellent attention to detail - all seams are encased, neatly sewn to accommodate 4-way stretching, and there are no loose threads. This dress can be hemmed if desired. * The soft and satiny fabric has an eye-catching, subtle sheen to it, adding to its beauty and attractiveness. Fabric is made of 92% Polyester for strength and 8% Spandex for elasticity. * The really unique advantage of this amazing, patented gown is that you have 72+ bodice styles to choose from. Go online to You Tube at home or in the library and check out the videos that offer tutorials on bodice-tying variations. You are only limited by your imagination. When practicing, it's to have a buddy behind you to help you with draping and tying. I practiced alone in front of a full-length mirror with a hand-held mirror assisting me in viewing the back. Bringing all the bridesmaids dresses to your Bachelorette party and experimenting is even more fun. * Everybody has a different shape and size, and being able to customize a dress to your own flattering style and preferences is a real asset. Different bodice designs bring a lovely variety to the wedding party.... or you can all wear the same style bodice and wear that unique dress in different complementary colors. * There are 30 gorgeous, elegant colors and unlimited styles from which to choose and/or invent yourself. * Style-wise, the two gently pleated, slightly overlapping bodice pieces that rise up from the waist are a generous 12" in width and 78" (6-1/2') in length. That gives you an amazing amount of fabric to drape, twist, wrap and tie in unlimited ways around your neck, waist and upper body. If you don't require that much fabric in bodice ties, develop your own "haute couture." You can neatly cut some fabric off to fashion hair ties, weaves, bows, small drawstring bags and more to complement your outfit. * This is a "one-size fits all" dress, recommended for US sizes 0-16. I wear a size 16, as I am big-boned, 5'8", 38B and 155 lbs. The dress fits me perfectly. I am probably near the upper limit as far as elasticized waist, length and bandeau fit go. * The included tube top/bandeau (for added modesty) that arrived with my 0-16 dress measures 12' x 14" and may offer little support for oversized girls. Consider purchasing a complementary bandeau with more support. * This Eye Candy formal dress can be worn repeatedly to different events by varying the bodice style. If you need a long dress for one occasion and then tire of it, you can shorten the longer dress to knee or cocktail length for a different occasion. The cut-off fabric could make a beautiful matching cape or bolero jacket. * One Size Plus is available for women who wear US 18-24. Once again, large-chested women may want to purchase their own sturdy, coordinating bandeau that offers more support and coverage than the skinny tube top offered for free with the dress. * An ideal dress for pregnant women, the stretchy fabric drapes and flows well and follows the contour of the body. Just place the elastic waistband over your baby bump. After delivery, the dress still fits. It is especially useful if you are nursing. No need to buy additional maternity wear for a formal occasion. * The gorgeous Teal fabric is True to Color. The fabric is stretchy and is non-see-through on me. Of course, fabric stretch increases the degree of see-through. If so, a half-slip can be worn underneath for more coverage if desired. * The dress hangs, drapes and flows with movement beautifully. * Ideal dress for many occasions as it takes up little space when neatly folded - a "travel" gown that resists wrinkles, prom, wedding, evening out, party, maternity, cruise-wear, award recognition night and more. *Recommended hand wash only in cold water for this non-shrink fabric. CONS: * NO INSTRUCTIONS INCLUDED! You Tube videos and other online info to the rescue! ... Or use your own wonderful imagination! SUMMARY: Fashionable, inexpensive, cool and comfortable, easy care, adjusts perfectly to weight changes, limitless style possibilities.... all in one beautiful dress. As you read the many reviews, think about the $45 you are spending. This is not a $150 to $200 dress. If that's in your budget, then look elsewhere. For $43 this is an incredibly good-looking and good value for the money! Enjoy! Highly Recommended.

No need to buy additional maternity wear for a formal occasion. * The gorgeous Teal fabric is True to Color. The fabric is stretchy and is non-see-through on me. Of course, fabric stretch increases the degree of see-through. If so, a half-slip can be worn underneath for more coverage if desired. * The dress hangs, drapes and flows with movement beautifully. * Ideal dress for many occasions as it takes up little space when neatly folded - a "travel" gown that resists wrinkles, prom, wedding, evening out, party, maternity, cruise-wear, award recognition night and more. *Recommended hand wash only in cold water for this non-shrink fabric. CONS: * NO INSTRUCTIONS INCLUDED! You Tube videos and other online info to the rescue! ... Or use your own wonderful imagination! SUMMARY: Fashionable, inexpensive, cool and comfortable, easy care, adjusts perfectly to weight changes, limitless style possibilities.... all in one beautiful dress. As you read the many reviews, think about the $45 you are spending. This is not a $150 to $200 dress. If that's in your budget, then look elsewhere. For $43 this is an incredibly good-looking and good value for the money! Enjoy! Highly Recommended.

Magnificent dress- pay attention to sizing- The color is not as bright as pictured- it’s not as bright a purple. It’s much darker. It is very fitted through the chest- and the dress is quite long. I’m 5’ 6”- it hit the ground. I wore flats in the wedding picture because my cousin, sister, and bride are shorter than me. The length was perfect for me with 3” heels. My cousin and sister are about 2” shorter- and it dragged- they wore heels- and it still dragged a bit. The material is awesome- and I would suggest this a perfect winter church/wedding dress. Keep in mind if you have animals or brush up against anything- the dress will cling to everything- ie- you need a lint roller. And finally the fit- I’m 36H/38G bra size- in most shirts, I can wear a size large (12-14). I’m a size 16/18 in pants- I bought the Xl in the dress. It fit great through the hip because of the natural flow. It was tight through the chest and natural through the waist (the Picts shown without spanx- which will give a more flattering look)- I think it fit perfectly for my size. The neckline was higher than expected- no cleavage- which is perfect for a church dress/ formal occasion. My sister is a 32DDD- she wears a size 10 pants. She wore the size large in the dress- it fit her great too- I’d size based on your bust more than your hips- ie if you have smaller or fuller hips- the shape of the dress camouflages it. Base your size primarily on your chest and waist. The material gives, and it became more comfortable the longer it was worn. It is a very pretty dress that can easily be dressed up for special occasions.

Like some said the side slits do have overlapping material and when sitting down or a good gust of wind while walking- it shows a high thigh but I didn’t mind showing some leg!! Elegant, quality dress that's easy to wear, in great color The dress is beautiful. My daughter wore it for a holiday performance, and it looked lovely both in person and on TV. The green is VERY dark -- can look almost black in some photos -- but that wasn't a problem, just be aware. We also ordered the same dress in the blue as a possible option, but it was a bright blue, not a deep sapphire. It was still pretty, but the color was not as good with my daughter's coloring. (Blue would look better on a blond or on someone with deeper skin tone.) The dress is very easy to wear; it is a stretch velvet. Fabric is soft and comfy but substantial quality. We do recommend getting a strapless slip of some kind as you can't wear a regular bra. Also, the slip allows you to go without pantyhose -- everything is nice and smooth -- a nice benefit -- everyone hates wearing pantyhose! The dress is formfitting and stretchy, so you want to mitigate panty lines, etc. My daughter is 5' 6" and the dress had to be shortened by about an inch; she wore 2-inch heels. If you're wearing higher heels, you may not have to get it shortened. Overall, a great buy that arrived 2 days earlier than they said it would. I would definitely buy from this place again!

Overall, I would probably order it again.

But both dresses booty sequins are unaligned. So disappointed.

The entire appeal of this dress hinges on how it fits! The dress is very cute once you find the correct fit. Amazon recommended size medium for me (I’m 5’5”, 135-140lbs, 34-DD bra). Usually Amazon’s recommendation is correct but this time it was not. Yes, the medium was too big but I almost didn’t reorder the dress because of how terrible the dress looked when it didn’t fit. Usually, if I get a size too big, I can at least tell that it would look good if it only fit. For this style dress, the entire appeal is in how it fits. Consider sizing down and if you do happen to purchase the dress in a size too big, give the dress a chance in the correct size! I’m happy I did because it’s actually very cute when it hugs the body! Comfortable, stretchy, soft, with a bit of sparkle—which I love! These pictures are kind of terrible, but I hope you can see the difference in size and how it makes the dress hang overall.

So cute and flattering The dress is very flattering, even if you have a little bit of a belly and the way it’s cut on the shoulders, make my arms look more toned. The coral is a very vibrant, orange pink. I think this color looks very good on fair or dark skin. For reference I am 5’5”, 153 pounds, 36B, with a 31” waist , and 40” hips. I went back-and-forth on deciding between a medium and a large. Ultimately, I ordered the medium and I’m glad that I did. Even though the sizing chart says it will fit a waist between 28.7” and 30.2” and hips 39”-40”. I found that it can do a bit more than what was stated because theres a stretch to the lining of the dress. The only place that has a thin elastic band is right under the bust and the waist definitely flounces out. So there is a lot more forgiveness than you would think. In my photos, I lifted the layers so you can see how the dress is divided into different layers with the unders slip and how it allows for a little bit more wiggle room of the hips and waist.

Very flattering, lots of compliments The dress is very flattering. Material is of decent quality. My only complaint is that it is difficult to get wrinkles out of. After steaming and ironing, I gave up. I received tons of compliments. I am 5’9”, approximately 210 lbs, wide hips, bubble butt, and a bloaty belly which I am insecure about, and I felt very comfortable. I purchased Apricot New in size 1XL plus and it was perfectly true to size and color. I do have more of an hourglass mid section so I could have probably gone down a size for a bit more fitted-fit there.

Gorgeous Dress but few issues I had to fix... The Dress itself is beautiful however it was way too big for me so I had to get it fixed before wearing. Comfortable, but not overly flattering. The dress itself is very comfortable. The sheer outer layer has a pretty print. The under layer is form fitting and rides up a little as you walk. If you don’t want any unwanted curves to show, I would pass on this dress. As I am 5’4”, this dress sits fine for me lengthwise, but on taller people it will be on the shorter side. What I thought I would really like, the sheer 3/4 sleeves, just doesn’t look right on me. The way the sleeve crosses over, makes it flare out funny. This might sound strange, but when it is on, the contrast of the body against the sleeve just looks off somehow. I actually think it would be prettier with a short sleeve as a result. The overall flow isn’t what I thought it would be. I am sure this will be different for different body types though. It is OK, not great.

Lovely The eggplant color and fabric is gorgeous! The cowl neckline is wide enough to be worn off the shoulders and drapes beautifully across the front, back and shoulders. A beautiful jewel embellishes the horizontal pleated bodice at the waistline above the gatherings of the skirt. The pleated bodice is very comfortable! The skirt folds beautifully down the front moves freely when walking. I hardly noticed the slit which starts above the knee. The back of the dress lays very smooth. Perfect length for a 5’7”-5’8” woman wearing a 1” platform or 2-3” heel shoe. I chose a different style Alex Evening dress in a size14 for a wedding which i also like.

Great dress for price! The eggplant color is so pretty. It's a dark purplish maroon color. I ordered up a size so I could sit and dance comfortably at my son's wedding. I'm 5ft 4in and usually a size 18. Im a chunky hour glass shape. The 20 fits perfectly! The jacket fabric is a little bit scratchy (nothing I can't tolerate for one evening) but the dress is lined with a soft, super stretchy lining and it drapes very comfortably. The sequin design adds a touch of elegance so you feel like your wearing a comfy sundress but "kicked up a notch". For a dress that will only be worn once or twice, and costs less than $150, you will be happy with this purchase! The gemstones are glued on so you must hand wash it. If you return it. I do not think they will refund the $16 shipping fee. I do not regret this purchase!!

Good quality, great price and very pretty The embroidered tulle overlay is beautiful and well made. There aren't any loose strings and the embroidery was done well. The lining is heavy and is a pretty eggshell blue. I dropped a star because it does fit a little oddly around the armpit and chest area. I am a 30 DDD and ordered a small. There is still a little bit of extra material that creates a small gap between my armpit and my rib cage. I can pull the dress lower onto my ribs to flatten it out. But it doesn't last once I start moving around. I'm hoping wearing a different bra may help fix the issue. I still feel this is a great dress, especially for the price.

The empire waist, flowy skirt and sleeves are all classics in plus size dresses for a reason. Really comfortable, flattering, fun, and easy to dance in. I'm 5'10" and it was long enough to wear with heels. The sequin details on the bodice sparkled in the sun. Super happy!

Very forgiving cut The empire waistline is flattering. The lil belt thing is ok...a bit of distraction for the tummy. The green is a true forest green...would be nice for Christmas. Meh The fabric and embroidery is surprisingly good for such an inexpensive dress, albeit it quite thin. The length is too short though, and I’m only 5’4. The biggest issue with the dress for me, though, is that the top part goes up too high and looks kinda frumpy.

Thick fabric The fabric does not feel thin/cheap. It feels like good quality and could last a few wears. The built in bra, to me, felt like it gives very little support. The strap was VERY loose on me and appears it’s designed for a larger breasted person. For reference I wear a 40D. The flowy sleeve were very comfortable. The slit was also not too revealing as I initially thought. However, the sizing is a bit weird. According to the sizing chart I was between a size 24X and 26X. For reference I normally wear a size 2-3X (20-22) and am 5’8” tall. On me, the 26X was very big. I felt like I was swimming in the dress. I think I could easily go down at least 1-2 sizes. I had ordered both sizes as their shipping said it would take a while and didn’t want to end up without a dress for my event. The seller did message me. They asked if I meant to order both dresses. I let them know that yes I did since I was in between sizes andn wasn’t sure if the smaller one would fit. They told me that if I returned one of the dresses I would have to pay for the shipping back to China (although that is not stated) that is I was sure I wanted to purchase both sizes. I let them know my measurements and they recommended I get the 26X or they could make me a custom dress. The custom dress was obviously final sale. I told them I would just take the larger size. They did ask when I needed my dress. I let them know and the shipping was faster than stated. I know have to buy another dress that is smaller and hope it gets here before the event.

Totally worth it. The fabric is a nice weight, heavy enough to have structure lightly enough to be comfortable. Pretty well made, I like the four fold interior binding at the neckline and armholes. This keeps the lace from getting scratchy on your skin and also gives it a very clean finish. The fit was spot on to what we expected, I did a small "petite alteration" at the shoulder seam because my daughter is only 5 ft, but it was easily achieved and the basic material of the dress was structurally sound enough for the alterations. (Not always the case) We added a petticoat for fullness. Little miss is wearing an XS, the skirt length from waist to hem is 23.5" and her petticoat was 24 in. It's was a separate item that sat on her waist so they're not going to align perfectly and she had about 2 in of tulle showing which was perfect for us. It's a nice true black, Slim A-line. 48" sweep along the curve (flat)

Nice, but longer than expected The fabric is nice and soft but thick enough to feel good quality. The color is true and beautiful. Not sure I'm loving the poufy shoulders that look more poufy on me than in the pictures, but I'll live with them. My only complaint is that it is way longer than I expected, which makes it look a bit frumpy on me. The pictures show it coming just below the knee, but on me (5'6" wearing a small) it comes half way down my calf. I think it would be cuter a bit shorter, maybe I'll get it taken up. Other than that, the dress fits nicely. The sleeves are nice and long and the elastic at the end is not too tight to be uncomfortable. The empire waist fits well and the v neck is not too deep to be awkward. Size seems true. I am 5'6", 135lbs, 36C bust and the small fits perfectly. Other than the awkward length (and poufy shoulders that I'm not sure about), this is a nice dress that will be a versatile addition to my wardrobe.

Elegant looking dress The fabric is nice and this is definitely a good dress for summer, the satin material has a good weight to it, so it flows well when walking and doesn't ride up or cling. I'm 5'11" and the hem of the dress hits around the middle of my calf. The elastic in the arms was a bit annoying at first but you get used to it quite quickly. The top of the dress is lined which is also a nice touch. This dress would be perfect to wear for attending a summer wedding. I'd say the price is pretty reasonable.

What a fabulous dress! The fabric is nice. Has some stretch to it too and not shiny. I did want a bit narrower shoulder strap just for my personal preference and did a little hand pleated stitch but it was also great as it came. I'm 5'2" and was perfect length with some low heels on. It went to the floor. Would look even better if you were a bit taller but still worked great for me. You feel pretty amazing walking around in it.

Wrong color The fabric is silky, not stiff like the black dress pictured in the reviews. It’s flattering on, but I ordered lilac and received a mauve pink which is a bummer because I hate pink but I need the dress for an upcoming event so it’s too late to get a return and replacement and no guarantee it will be the right color. That being said, I’m 5’7 and 160lbs and it fits perfectly, a little loose but that’s good for a dress like this, you don’t want it too tight or it won’t drape properly. If you’re worried it will be small order a size up, better to order a little large than too small. When you tie it around the back it makes it difficult to raise your arms but that’s just weird construction. The waistline is also a lot higher than pictured but it’s a 40$ dress. It could be way worse but I’ll definitely wear this and maybe order more colors or find a company that makes these with a lower waistline.

The fabric is sort of like satin. As a result, it was incredibly static-prone. If you have pets, you will have fur all over it. It also bunched up a lot until we sprayed it with a bit of water to neutralize the static. However, I don't think you'll be able to wear this item with pantyhose or body shapers unless you rub anti-static cling sheets all over it. The top is fairly generous and stretchy. The medium dress would easily fit a B to D cup bust. The bottom half is less generous and not stretchy. If you are a pear shape and in between sizes, I would recommend sizing up. The black medium dress did NOT have a liner, contrary to what I saw on another reviewer's video. But it was not see through and was fine without one. The sleeves can be worn comfortably up or down below shoulders. The hemline came down mid calf, but rises to just cover the knees when you sit down. My daughter in the pictures is 5'2" and 120 lbs and the medium dress fits great.

Doesn’t Really Look as Advertised The material doesn’t look the same, and the color is a bit off. The neck is really big and doesn’t look like it does on the model. Additionally, the part at the stomach where it goes from floofed to fitted looks good on the model, however, on the actual dress, it is just a piece of elastic and it isn’t very secure. Some people may prefer the elastic band because it creates a bit more wiggle room for size and could be really nice. It is definitely comfy! I ordered a small (Bust: 36”, Waist: 26”, Hips: 36”) and as you can see from the photos, it wasn’t that small. It had no shape to it naturally and it looked very unflattering. I also include photos of how it *could* look if altered. However, it was a $42 dress so I didn’t want to mess with the materials through alterations. The company was very kind and allowed me to return the dress, and I have had no problem with them. They are a good a trustworthy company. The dress itself isn’t even that horrendously off from the ad, but it wasn’t what I was looking for. I am a bridesmaid and, while it might be cute for a night out, it didn’t seem elegant enough in person to be suitable as a bridesmaids dress.
